header.overviewDesc: Service status, logs and quick actions

system.title: System Requirements
system.gitDesc: Version control for MCP & skill repos
system.allReady: All prerequisites are installed and ready
system.specifyPath: Installed but not detected? Specify path

security.warning: Security Warning: Your OpenClaw version ({{version}}) is insecure.
security.require: A version >= 2026.1.29 is required. Please update immediately.

compaction.title: Compaction & Memory
compaction.enableCompactionDesc: Compress conversation history when it gets too long
compaction.tokenThresholdDesc: Number of tokens before compaction triggers
compaction.contextPruningDesc: Limit the number of recent messages kept in context
compaction.maxMessagesDesc: Maximum number of recent messages to keep

subagent.title: Subagent Defaults
subagent.desc: Global limits for nested subagent spawning
subagent.maxTotalSizeDesc: Limit across standard session attachment drops

config.title: Configuration Management
config.validSuccess: Configuration is valid according to the CLI schema.
config.importConfirm: Importing configuration will overwrite your current settings. Continue?
config.importSuccess: Configuration imported successfully! Please restart the manager to apply all changes.

browser.title: Browser Control
browser.enableBrowserDesc: Allow agents to browse the web
browser.chromeColorDesc: Custom color for the browser window

tools.title: Tools & Security
tools.desc: Manage tool access profiles and settings
tools.securityProfileDesc: Configures baseline allowlist for built-in tools across all agents

webSearch.title: Web Search
webSearch.braveApiKeyDesc: Required for agents to perform web searches

managerUpdate.title: Manager Update
managerUpdate.desc: Keep OpenClaw Manager up to date
managerUpdate.checkForUpdatesDesc: Check GitHub for the latest Manager version

danger.title: Danger Zone
danger.uninstallConfirm: Are you sure? This will permanently delete the entire
danger.uninstallConfirmFolder: folder (all configs, agents, and data) and uninstall the OpenClaw CLI.

aiConfig.title: AI Model Configuration
aiConfig.desc: Manage AI providers and models used by OpenClaw
aiConfig.note2: Supports official Providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Kimi, etc.) and custom OpenAI/Anthropic compatible APIs
aiConfig.note3: The primary model is used for Agent's default inference and can be switched at any time
aiConfig.note4: Restart the service for configuration changes to take effect

dialog.providerNameEditWarning: Provider name cannot be modified. Please delete and recreate if you need to change it.
dialog.customUrlWarning: You are using an official Provider name but have modified the API URL. It is recommended to use a different name to avoid configuration conflicts.
dialog.apiKeyPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep existing API Key, or enter a new one
dialog.apiKeyHint: Leave empty if you don't need to change the API Key
dialog.ollamaMissing: Ollama was not detected on your system. You'll need to install it to run models locally.
dialog.installNewModel: Install a new model (e.g., qwen3.5:9b, llama3)
dialog.downloading: Downloading model... this may take a few minutes depending on your internet connection and model size.

channels.title: Message Channels
channels.selectChannel: Select a channel on the left to configure

telegram.name: Telegram
telegram.help:
1. Search @BotFather and send /newbot to get Token
2. Search @userinfobot to get User ID

discord.name: Discord
discord.help: Get from Discord Developer Portal, enable Developer Mode to copy Channel ID

slack.name: Slack
slack.help: Get from Slack API dashboard, Channel ID can be copied from channel details

feishu.name: Feishu
feishu.help: Get credentials from Feishu Open Platform, Chat ID can be found in group settings

imessage.name: iMessage
imessage.help: macOS only, requires Messages access permission

whatsapp.name: WhatsApp
whatsapp.help: Requires QR code scan to login

multiBot.title: Multi-bot mode active
multiBot.desc: Bot Token, DM Policy, Group Policy, and Stream Mode are now configured per-account below.
multiBot.noBots: No bot accounts configured. Your existing bot token is used as a single agent.
multiBot.addMultiple: Add multiple bots to route each to a different agent.
multiBot.exclusiveTopicsDesc: Comma-separated Topic IDs (e.g. 42, 45). Leave empty to allow all.
multiBot.saveHint: Each bot account can be bound to a different agent via Agents → Routing Rules using Account ID.
multiBot.agentsHint: Each group gets its own settings. Use system prompts to give each group a unique personality/domain.
multiBot.exclusiveTopicsHint: If set, bot will only respond in these topics and ignore all others.
multiBot.useExclusiveTopics: Use Exclusive Topics above to restrict this bot to specific topics.
multiBot.noBotAccounts: No bot accounts configured. Your existing bot token is used as a single agent.
multiBot.addMultipleBots: Add multiple bots to route each to a different agent.

whatsapp.qrDesc: WhatsApp requires QR code scan to login
whatsapp.loginHint: Please scan the QR code in the popup terminal window to complete login. The interface will update automatically after successful login.
whatsapp.afterLoginHint: After successful login, click the button on the right to refresh status, or run: openclaw channels login --channel whatsapp

feishu.pluginDesc: Feishu channel requires the @m1heng-clawd/feishu plugin to be installed first.
feishu.manualInstall: Or run manually: openclaw plugins install @m1heng-clawd/feishu

groups.label: Allowed Groups (Chat ID)
groups.noGroups: No groups added. Bot will ignore all groups.
groups.systemPromptPlaceholder: e.g. You are an investment analyst. Your research is in investments/.
groups.eachGroupHint: Each group gets its own settings. Use system prompts to give each group a unique personality/domain.

groupSenders.label: Allowed Senders in Groups (User ID)
groupSenders.noSenders: No senders restricted. All group members can interact.
groupSenders.hint: Saved as channels.telegram.groupAllowFrom. Numeric Telegram user IDs.

dmAllowlist.placeholderAuto: Users will be added automatically via pairing flow.
dmAllowlist.label: Allowed DM Users (User ID)
dmAllowlist.fetchTitle: Fetch users who have messaged this bot
dmAllowlist.savedAs: Saved per-account as allowFrom. Inherited from primary bot if empty.
dmAllowlist.noUsersFound: No users found. Make sure someone has sent a message to this bot first (e.g. /start).
dmAllowlist.placeholderPairing: Users added via pairing flow. You can also add manually.
dmAllowlist.placeholderAllowlist: No users allowed. Add user IDs above.

placeholder.testChannelOptional: Channel ID for sending test messages (optional)
placeholder.testChatOptional: Group/User ID for sending test messages (optional)

agents.title: Agent Routing
agents.noAgents: No agents configured. Create one to get started.
agents.routingDesc: Route incoming messages to specific agents
agents.noRules: No routing rules configured. Messages will use the default agent.

dialog.subagentsDesc: Select which other agents this agent can spawn as subagents.
dialog.noSubagents: No other agents available. Create more agents first.
dialog.channelHint: Currently only Telegram supports multi-agent routing.
dialog.selectBotDesc: Select the Telegram bot account to link with a new agent.
dialog.configureAgentDesc: Configure the agent that will handle messages for

mcp.title: Model Context Protocol
mcp.desc: Manage MCP servers to extend Agent capabilities
mcp.mcporterRequired: mcporter is required for MCP support
mcp.mcporterDesc1: OpenClaw can use MCP servers via the mcporter skill
mcp.mcporterDesc2: Install mcporter to enable MCP server integration with OpenClaw agents
mcp.noServersDesc: Add a server manually or install from GitHub

dialog.installFromGitDesc: Install from a GitHub repository URL
dialog.pluginDesc: Uses OpenClaw's native plugin system. Only works for packages with openclaw.extensions in package.json.
dialog.sourceDesc: Clones the repository, runs npm install & build. Works with any MCP server from GitHub.
dialog.serverUrlDesc: HTTP/HTTPS endpoint for the remote MCP server
dialog.commandPlaceholder: e.g. node, python, or absolute path to executable

skills.clawhubDescInstalled: The official CLI tool is installed and ready.
